name: Fruited Turkey Roll
recipeIngredient: 1 package Low Sodium Instant Chicken Broth
1 tsp Ginger root, peeled and minced
2 tbsp Wondra Flour or other flour
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix the egg with the ground turkey and bread crumbs.
Add basil, oregano, and sage.
2. Roll out the meat mixture between two sheets of plastic wrap or waxed paper to about 8x12 inches.
3. Remove the top sheet. Mix Dijon mustard and apricot preserves (heat if necessary) and spread over one side of the meat.
4. In a large skillet, sauté mushrooms in olive oil until lightly browned. Add tarragon during the last minute of cooking.
5. Layer the sautéed mushrooms and onion (sautéed until tender) over three-quarters of the meat.
6. Scatter cheese over half of the meat.
7. Roll up the meat in a jelly-roll fashion to make an 8-inch log. Place seam side down in a lightly greased oven-proof shallow baking pan.
This can be made ahead to this point and kept covered in the refrigerator for several hours.
8. In the same pan used for sautéing onions and mushrooms, sauté ginger root and garlic over high heat for about 30 seconds to 1 minute—do not brown the garlic.
9. If needed, add a little more oil, flour, pepper, and chicken broth to the pan.
10. Cook the roux for 1 minute and immediately thin with slow additions of nectar and water.
11. Add vermouth, fruits, and cook until the fruit plumps and the sauce thickens slightly.
12. Transfer the meat to a serving platter and spoon over the fruit sauce. Pass extra sauce alongside.
